question.text: A Qualified RTA (QRTA) servicing Mutual Funds investors must have Investor Service Centers in at least how many cities based on investor population?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** QRTAs servicing Mutual Funds investors must have Investor Service Centers in at least 100 cities based on investor population pertaining to the Mutual Funds clients they service. **The Trap:** 50 cities seems adequate for national coverage but the SEBI requirement is 100 cities, reflecting the widespread distribution of mutual fund investors across India. **Cross-Exam Context:** The 100-city requirement for QRTAs parallels the mutual fund industry's efforts to expand beyond top 30 cities (B-30 initiatives). This is relevant for NISM Series V-A and V-B exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** QRTAs must also conduct annual stakeholder/investor satisfaction surveys and publish results on their website before March 31 every year. They must also maintain technical capacity for twice the peak transaction load.
